prompt: During this morning's flag-service outage every single web worker hung for the FULL wait_ready timeout at boot and then behaved as if the warmup had timed out — even though the warmup thread had already died within milliseconds (its ConnectionError was sitting right there in error() the whole time). Rolling restarts took 40x longer than they should have. When the flag service is up, boots are instant, so it's specifically the failure path that stalls. The contract is spelled out in flag_cache.py's docstring: wait_ready returns True as soon as the attempt has FINISHED, success or failure. Repro: python3 test_flag_cache.py. Fix flag_cache.py; do not touch the test file.
